log_entries.thought_preview: The sample overview confirms this is a UPX-packed PE executable with tags indicating it's a spreader with persistence capabilities. I now need to get all IOC types to understand the full scope of indi

I have confirmed the IOC types available. The sample shows 2 domains, 7 persistence registry keys, 3 dropped files, and 1 process created. I need to investigate the network activity to see the full DN

The network activity shows two suspicious domain lookups (85746lolo.cn and 87363lulu.cn) with no resolved IPs, suggesting potential C2 or beaconing infrastructure. Now I need to examine the dropped fi

The dropped files show three executables: cftmon.exe (appears twice in different locations) and spools.exe. The spools.exe is particularly suspicious as it's a common name used for masquerading. I nee

The registry changes reveal a sophisticated persistence mechanism. The malware uses multiple techniques: 1) Winlogon Shell/UserInit manipulation (classic rootkit technique), 2) Run keys for autoload a
The process activity shows the malware uses SetFileTime (anti-forensics) and loads various system DLLs including network-related ones (ws2_32, wininet, rasapi32). The execution chain shows it deletes
